message:
fix(connectors): Improve message ordering determinism across all connectors

Fix non-deterministic message ordering that could cause inconsistent
search results and index churn during re-indexing. Each connector now
uses stable sort keys with proper tie-breaking.

OpenCode connector (src/connectors/opencode.rs):
- Add PartInfo.index field with aliases (order, sequence) for part ordering
- Implement sort_parts_for_message() to order parts within a message
- Use (timestamp, message_id) tuple for deterministic message ordering
- Collect messages into pending buffer before sorting
- Ensure consistent ordering even when timestamps are missing or equal

ChatGPT connector (src/connectors/chatgpt.rs):
- Fix timestamp comparison to handle None values explicitly
- Add message_id tie-breaking when timestamps match
- Use match-based ordering: Some/Some, Some/None, None/Some, None/None
- Simplify started_at/ended_at tracking with Option::or()
- Remove redundant if-else chains

Cline connector (src/connectors/cline.rs):
- Add stable secondary sort key for equal timestamps
- Ensure deterministic ordering across re-indexes

Codex connector (src/connectors/codex.rs):
- Add message ID fallback for timestamp ties
- Document ordering guarantees

Gemini connector (src/connectors/gemini.rs):
- Improve timestamp parsing robustness
- Add tie-breaking on message sequence

These changes ensure that:
1. Re-indexing the same data produces identical results
2. Message order is consistent across different runs
3. Missing timestamps don't cause undefined ordering
4. Search result rankings are stable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
